QF.id: VB Concept and Role of Mutual Fund_5

question.text: In an open-ended mutual fund scheme, when can an investor buy or sell (redeem) units?

answer.text: On any business day at the prevailing NAV
Only during the initial launch period (NFO)
Only at the end of a fixed maturity period
Only once a month on a fixed date

explanation.text: **The Logic:** An open-ended fund allows investors to buy (subscribe) or sell (redeem) their units on any business day at the current day's NAV. There is no fixed lock-in period or maturity date. This gives investors full flexibility and liquidity. **The Trap:** Buying only during NFO is a feature of close-ended schemes, not open-ended. Open-ended funds are available for transactions every business day. **Cross-Exam Context:** This is also tested in Series V-A (MF Distributors) under fund types. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Open-ended funds are the most common and most suitable for retired clients because of their liquidity. If a client needs money urgently, they can redeem their units and typically receive money within 1-3 business days.
